entities.name: BOTRYTIS GREY MOULD
entities.description: An economically important disease of chickpea caused by Botrytis cinerea Pers. ex. Fr.

entities.name: BOTRYTIS CINEREA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The host plant (Cicer arietinum L.) affected by Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: COOL CLOUDY AND HUMID WEATHER
entities.description: Environmental conditions that favor the persistence and spread of Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of chickpea accessions to resist or tolerate Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: CHEMICAL FUNGICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used to manage and control Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: CONTROLLED-ENVIRONMENT AND FIELD-SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques developed for the identification of host plant resistance in chickpea germplasm.

entities.name: CICER SPP
entities.description: Cultivated and wild species of the chickpea genus evaluated for disease resistance.

entities.name: DISEASE PREDICTIVE MODELS
entities.description: Models used to enable precision-based application of fungicides.

entities.name: INTEGRATED DISEASE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A comprehensive approach combining multiple management components to control Botrytis grey mould.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where large-scale on-farm studies of Integrated Disease Management were conducted.

entities.name: NEPAL
entities.description: A country where large-scale on-farm studies of Integrated Disease Management were conducted.

entities.name: BANGLADESH
entities.description: A country where large-scale on-farm studies of Integrated Disease Management were conducted.
